results.title: AI Systems & Real Estate Innovation – Monarch Internship & Co-op | Old Dominion University
highlights:
AI Systems & Real Estate Innovation – Monarch Internship & Co-op | Old Dominion University

The Ron Sawyer Team

# AI Systems & Real Estate Innovation

- Share This:
- Copy Link

We are looking for a college student or emerging professional who is already actively learning and using AI tools and wants real-world experience applying AI inside a growing business environment.

This paid summer internship is designed for someone who understands the potential of AI beyond casual use and is excited about helping a real estate company build practical AI systems, workflows, automations, and operational improvements.

You’ll work directly with leadership to help identify inefficiencies, explore AI-driven solutions, and assist in building systems that improve productivity, organization, marketing, communication, and operations.

#### This is an opportunity to gain:

● Hands-on AI implementation experience ● Real-world business and operations exposure ● Real estate industry knowledge ● Experience working on meaningful projects that may continue beyond the internship ● Potential long-term employment opportunities

#### What You’ll Work On

● Researching and testing AI platforms and tools ● Helping create automations and AI-assisted workflows ● Organizing and streamlining operational processes ● Assisting with AI-enhanced marketing and communication systems ● Exploring integrations between existing systems and AI tools ● Helping leadership better understand practical AI implementation opportunities

#### Who We’re Looking For

We are specifically looking for someone who: ● Is already actively learning, studying, or experimenting with AI ● Understands tools like OpenAI ChatGPT, automation platforms, prompting, or AI-assisted workflows ● Thinks creatively about efficiency and problem-solving ● Is naturally curious and proactive ● Can explain technology concepts in a practical business setting ● Wants to help build and improve systems — not just complete assigned tasks

#### Bonus Skills

● Prompt engineering ● Workflow automation tools ● CRM or database familiarity ● Spreadsheet/data organization skills ● Marketing or content creation experience ● Interest in entrepreneurship, operations, or real estate

#### Internship Details

● Paid internship ● Summer position ● Flexible scheduling for students ● Potential for continued employment after the internship

#### Location

Hampton Roads / Chesapeake area with possible hybrid flexibility.

#### To Apply

Please send: ● Your resume ● A brief introduction explaining your experience with AI tools and what interests you about this opportunity ● Any examples of AI projects, automations, prompts, or systems you’ve worked on (optional but encouraged)

results.title: Technical Customer Solutions Manager Intern – Fall 2026 – Monarch Internship & Co-op | Old Dominion University
highlights:
Technical Customer Solutions Manager Intern – Fall 2026 – Monarch Internship & Co-op | Old Dominion University
[...]
# Technical Customer Solutions Manager Intern – Fall 2026
[...]
At Amazon Web Services (AWS), we are working to be the most customer-centric company on earth. AWS is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform, offering over 200 fully featured services from data centers globally. Millions of customers, including the largest financial institutions in the world, are using AWS to lower costs, become more agile, and innovate faster. AWS is looking to hire and develop the next generation of leaders for our organization. We are seeking talented and enthusiastic students who are passionate about cloud technology, financial services, and helping large enterprises transform through innovation.
[...]
As a Customer Solutions Manager (CSM) Intern on the Global Financial Services (GFS) team, you will gain exposure to how AWS partners with the world’s largest financial institutions to drive cloud adoption at scale. Working alongside experienced CSMs, you will contribute to high-impact programs that shape how customers migrate workloads, modernize applications, and harness the power of generative AI and agentic AI to reimagine business processes. You will develop business acumen, build technical fluency in cloud computing, and learn how cross-functional teams at AWS collaborate to deliver results at scale. Your work will directly inform the strategies and deliverables that CSMs bring to executive-level customer engagements, at a time when financial services organizations are rapidly transforming their businesses in the age of Agentic AI.
[...]
- Develop cloud adoption strategies, migration roadmaps, and business case frameworks that CSMs leverage in customer engagements
- Research and evaluate generative AI and agentic AI use cases relevant to financial services and synthesize findings into actionable recommendations
- Conduct analysis on financial services industry trends, competitive landscapes, and emerging cloud and AI adoption patterns to strengthen customer narratives
[...]
- Contribute to internal tooling, playbooks, and best-practice frameworks that scale the impact of the CSM team, including leveraging AI/ML tools to improve team productivity
[...]
- Complete the AWS Cloud Practitioner, AI Practitioner and Solutions Architect Associate Certification while also participating in technical training, mentorship, and professional development events
[...]
- Has interest and aptitude to learn about cloud computing and AI/ML, with a “can-do” attitude and strong ownership
- Is curious about how generative AI and agentic systems are transforming the financial services industry
- Can think strategically about business challenges while also diving into technical details
- Is comfortable with ambiguity and can work independently in
[...]
paced environment
[...]
- Are enrolled in a academic program that is physically located in the United States
- Are 18 years of age or older
- Work 40 hours/week minimum and commit to 12 week internship
- Currently enrolled in a bachelor’s degree with a graduation conferral date between May 2027 and July 2027.
- Experience in one or more technology domains, including but not limited to: Networking fundamentals, Databases (Relational and/or NoSQL), Operating Systems (Unix, Linux, and/or Windows), AI/ML.
- Experience with Gen AI, Large Language Models (LLMs), Machine Learning (ML), etc
[...]
- Bachelor’s degree or above in computer science or other related discipline
- Experience implementing a cloud-based technology solution in a school project or while working for a company
- Experience with one or more of the following domains: analytics, security, storage, DevOps, application development, or machine learning
- Experience in program and project management fundamentals
- Knowledge of AWS or cloud computing

results.title: Senior Research Scientist/Scholar – School of Engineering, Architecture and Aviation, and the job with Hampton University | 37992107
highlights:
Job description: The Hampton University School of Engineering, Architecture and Aviation is seeking a candidate for a one-year joint appointment with the Division of Information Technology as a Senior Research Scientist/Scholar in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning. The position will be funded through a grant from Amazon Robotics (AR) and will be filled immediately or will remain open until it is filled. The position is located in Hampton, Virginia. Founded in 1868, Hampton University (www.hamptonu.edu) is located on approximately 300 acres of the Virginia Peninsula. It is a privately endowed, co-educational, nonsectarian institution of higher education and is fully accredited by the Southern Association of Colleges and Schools (SACS). Hampton University, a Historically Black College and University (HBCU), is enjoying tremendous growth and development from a traditional teaching institution to a more research-intensive institution. Hampton University is currently an R2 Carnegie classification institution of higher learning with a primarily undergraduate student population of approximately 4,200 students pursuing degrees in diverse areas of study. It is pertinent to note that Hampton University is a recipient of an Amazon Robotics (AR) grant to support its work on Robotics, Artificial Intelligence, and Machine Learning. The position is a one-year appointment that may be renewed subject to the availability of funds. Job Duties: The individual selected for this position will be expected to:
[...]
- Conduct independent and collaborative research in AI/ML, and closely related areas aligned with Amazon Robotics funding and research priorities.
[...]
- Lead and contribute to the design, implementation, and evaluation of AI/ML models, algorithms, and systems in support of sponsored research projects.
[...]
- Conduct advanced research and publish papers in high-quality peer-reviewed journals and conferences.
[...]
- Contribute to the development, preparation, and submission of grant proposals to federal agencies, foundations, and industry partners.
[...]
- Serve as principal investigator or co-investigator on externally funded research projects, including managing scientific aims, timelines, and deliverables.
[...]
- Mentor undergraduate research assistants engaged in AI/ML related projects.
[...]
- Collaborate with faculty across disciplines to develop and execute joint research initiatives, and labs focused on AI/ML and its applications.
[...]
- Engage in seminars and guest lectures in the candidate’s area of expertise.
[...]
- Participate in research development activities, including workshops, symposia, and strategic planning for AI/ML research.
[...]
Qualifications: The successful applicant must have a doctorate degree in either Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Information Technology, Artificial Intelligence, or a closely related field with expertise in one or more of the following areas: robotics, machine learning, reinforcement learning, and artificial intelligence. At least five years of academic research experience in AI/ML at an R-1 or R-2 institution is required. The successful candidate will possess the following skills and abilities:
[...]
- An established record of research: The candidate should have a strong track record of publishing high-quality research papers in top-tier journals or conferences.
[...]
- A deep understanding of AI/ML: The candidate should have a deep understanding of the theoretical foundations of AI/ML, as well as the latest advances in these fields. They should also be familiar with a variety of AI techniques.
[...]

results.title: Optum Hiring Data Scientist | Jobformore
highlights:
Optum Hiring Data Scientist | Jobformore
[...]
# Data Scientist
[...]
Optum is seeking a Data Science professional who is passionate about applying machine learning, Generative AI, and Large Language Models (LLMs) to solve complex healthcare problems on a scale. Imagine working with massive volumes of healthcare data spanning clinical, claims, behavioral, and digital interactions – powering intelligent personalization, discovery, and decision support for millions of members, providers, and care teams.
[...]
At Optum, the Data Science mission is to transform healthcare data into robust, scalable AI solutions, including LLM based and Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) systems that combine enterprise knowledge with advanced language models to deliver accurate, contextual, and trustworthy insights. These solutions help uncover deep usage patterns, member journeys, and care signals that inform and continuously evolve Optum’s digital products, clinical platforms, and healthcare services.
[...]
Your work will directly influence how healthcare consumers engage with digital experiences, how clinicians access timely insights, and how business and care strategies are shaped through data. By joining Optum’s data science team, you will be part of an exciting journey to build advanced ML and GenAI driven products, grounded in responsible AI principles, that operate at enterprise scale and create meaningful impact across the healthcare ecosystem.
[...]
- Design, develop, and deploy advanced analytics, machine learning, and Generative AI solutions to address complex healthcare and business problems using largescale structured and unstructured data
[...]
- Build and optimize LLM based applications, including Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) frameworks, to enable accurate, context aware, and explainable insights from enterprise healthcare data sources such as clinical notes, claims, provider data, and policy documents
[...]
- Develop, evaluate, and finetune machine learning and GenAI models, including prompt engineering, retrieval strategies, and model evaluation techniques to improve accuracy, relevance, and performance
[...]
- Translate complex analytical and GenAI outputs into
[...]
, actionable insights for
[...]
- Support prototyping, validation, and deployment of GenAI and ML solutions, working with engineering teams to ensure solutions are scalable, secure, and maintainable. Continuously stay current with advancements in LLMs, GenAI frameworks, and healthcare AI trends, contributing innovative ideas and improvements to Optum’s capabilities
[...]
### Required Qualifications:
[...]
- Bachelor’s Degree (preferably in information technology, engineering, math, computer science, analytics, engineering or other related field)
[...]
- 4+ years of experience in handling/active development in data science projects
[...]
- 4+ years of experience in Microsoft Azure Cloud, Databricks, Mlflow, Spark SQL & Python
[...]
- Experience using Machine Learning Algorithms: Linear & Logistic Regression, Time Series analysis, Support Vector Machine, K-Means clustering, K-Nearest Neighbors Decision Trees, Random Forest, Naive Bayes, PCA, SVD, Artificial Neural Networks, Association Rules, Genetic Algorithm, Optimization, Bagging and Boosting algorithms etc.
[...]
- Understanding of data, schema, data model, machine learning and how to bring efficiency in big data related life cycle
[...]
- Proven skills to create opportunities using machine learning or artificial intelligence
[...]
Optum, part of UnitedHealth Group, is a global health services and technology company revolutionizing healthcare delivery through data analytics, pharmacy benefits, and digital health solutions that connect millions to better care. Headquartered in Eden Prairie, Minnesota, Optum serves patients, providers, payers, and pharmacies worldwide with innovative platforms for care optimization, claims processing, and population health management.

results.title: Network Development Engineer Intern – Fall Intern – Monarch Internship & Co-op | Old Dominion University
highlights:
Network Development Engineer Intern – Fall Intern – Monarch Internship & Co-op | Old Dominion University

Amazon

# Network Development Engineer Intern – Fall Intern

- Share This:
- Copy Link

Do you want to be part of a team that deploys and automates one of the world’s largest and complex networks? As a Network Engineer working within AWS Infrastructure you will be responsible for deploying network infrastructure across all of our network fabrics that supports the Amazon Retail Service, Kindle, Payments and multiple AWS services. You will work closely with our automation teams in defining the tools that allow us to scale at unprecedented volume. You’ll have the opportunity to deploy, scale and automate our network across multiple global datacenter, including existing footprints and greenfield locations. If this sounds exciting to you – come build the future with us!

#### Key job responsibilities

- Create simple, repetitive deployment processes that increase both velocity and quality.
- Create and update our network standards and ensure that the network is deployed to these standards.
- Work closely with our internal customers on designs/solutions; bringing those designs/solutions from concept to production.

#### Basic Qualifications

- Work 40 hours/week minimum and commit to 12 week internship maximum
- Experience working in a Linux/Unix environment
- Experience in scripting languages such as Perl, Python, or Javascript
- Are 18 years of age or older
- Currently has, or is in the process of obtaining, a Bachelor’s degree or above in Computer Information Systems, Management Information Systems, Telecommunications or related fields
- Knowledge of IP networking fundamentals such as BGP, OSPF and other R&S technologies

#### Preferred Qualifications

- Knowledge of network hardware and packet forwarding architectures
- Experience handling ambiguous or undefined challenges through strong problem solving abilities

Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status.

Amazon internships are full-time positions, and interns should expect to work onsite Monday-Friday, up to 40 hours per week typically between 8am-5pm. Specific team norms around working hours will be communicated by your manager. Interns should not have conflicts such as classes or other employment during the Amazon work-day.

Applicants should have a minimum of one quarter/semester/trimester remaining in their studies after their internship concludes. By applying to this position, your application will be considered for all ASIC roles at all locations we hire for in the United States

results.title: Virtual Summer Internship – Monarch Internship & Co-op | Old Dominion University
highlights:
Virtual Summer Internship – Monarch Internship & Co-op | Old Dominion University

Teamship Hampton Roads

# Virtual Summer Internship

- Share This:
- Copy Link

🚀 Ready to build your career skills in a whole new way this summer?

Old Dominion University students are invited to participate in Teamship Hampton Roads, a unique virtual internship experience made possible through the Virginia Talent + Opportunity Partnership (V-TOP)!

🌊 What is Teamship? Teamship brings together diverse teams of college students from across Hampton Roads to solve real-world challenges for real businesses. Working alongside a certified Teamship coach, you’ll collaborate, research, design solutions, and pitch your ideas directly to industry partners.

This is more than an internship — it’s an opportunity to build the future-of-work skills employers are looking for.

✨ Why participate? • Gain a unique internship experience for your resume and LinkedIn • Work on real business challenges with regional employers • Collaborate with students from across Hampton Roads • Strengthen critical career mindsets: Analytical, Design, Collective, and Self-Aware • Learn and apply design-thinking strategies • Completely FREE for participating students

Teamship Hampton Roads Application

Teamship Hampton Roads is available to undergrad students attending a Hampton Roads-based higher ed institution. Seats are extremely limited, and application responses will be reviewed to determine program offers and waitlist placement. Please check out this document to preview the application questions, prepare them in advance, and have someone review them prior to submitting your application.

Estimated time to complete the application: 10-15 minutes

The priority application deadline is Monday, 5/25 at 11:59 pm. Since space is limited, applicants who apply by the priority registration date receive first consideration. All available seats may be filled from the priority applicant pool. Students who apply after May 25 may only be eligible for placement on the waitlist.

All applicants will be notified of their application status no later than one week after the final application deadline.

If you need assistance accessing this form, please contact ksollenberger@collegeboard.org.

Advances in AI, data, and computational sciences are transforming drug discovery and development. Roche’s Research and Early Development organisations at Genentech (gRED) and Pharma (pRED) have demonstrated how these technologies accelerate R&D, leveraging data and novel computational models to drive impact. Seamless data sharing and access to models across gRED and pRED are essential to maximising these opportunities. The new Computational Sciences Center of Excellence (CoE) is a strategic, unified group whose goal is to harness the transformative power of data and Artificial Intelligence (AI) to assist our scientists in both pRED and gRED to deliver more innovative and transformative medicines for patients worldwide. ​

We are seeking a Senior or Principal Machine Learning Scientist to join the Foundation Models team within Prescient Design (gRED). In this role, you will drive the development of our internal reasoning Large Language Models (LLMs) and enable it to succeed at drug discovery tasks, including biomolecular design. results.title: Class of 2026: Data Science Student Ready to Support Small Businesses and Underserved Communities Through Cybersecurity and AI | Old Dominion University
highlights:
Class of 2026: Data Science Student Ready to Support Small Businesses and Underserved Communities Through Cybersecurity and AI | Old Dominion University

# Class of 2026: Data Science Student Ready to Support Small Businesses and Underserved Communities Through Cybersecurity and AI

May 13, 2026

share

###### By Kelsey Kendall

For Makendra Crosby (B.S. ’24), data science and cybersecurity are not just about numbers and computers, they are about people.

As she prepares to graduate with her master’s in data science and analytics with a concentration in artificial intelligence and machine learning, she is focused on using her skills to serve others and make technology more accessible.

Old Dominion University already felt like home long before she enrolled. Growing up in Suffolk, she regularly visited campus for STEM camps and workshops. Now, as graduation approaches, that familiarity makes the moment feel especially meaningful.

“I’m really going to miss it,” Makendra said.

Not long after her first semester, Makendra stepped into a leadership role as a peer mentor for the Cybersecurity Living-Learning Community (LLC). There, she helped fellow students who shared the same major or interests navigate the transition from high school to college. As a peer mentor, she offered academic support, advice and helped them find their footing on campus.

Later, she served in the same position with the Cybersecurity Impact Learning Community, similar to the LLCs, but for students living off campus.

After graduating with her bachelor’s degree and starting work on her master’s degree, she transitioned into a graduate assistant role to lead new peer mentors.

“It was like second nature,” she said. “I get to supervise the student leaders who are in the same role I was in. It was fun, like a full circle moment.”

That commitment to supporting others extended beyond her mentorship roles. Makendra is a member of Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Incorporated; Phi Sigma Rho National STEM Sorority and the National Council of Negro Women. It was important to her to work with her peers to make an impact on the community. She helped organize regular drives to collect school supplies, personal care items and more for local organizations.

Alongside her leadership and service on campus, Makendra was awarded the U.S. Department of War SMART Scholarship, a highly competitive opportunity that supports students in STEM fields through funding and real-world experience. Through this opportunity, she interned at a U.S. Navy research facility, where she gained hands-on experience working in a professional and technical environment. It also provided a direct path into the workforce, asMakendra has secured a full-time position following graduation to continue building her career.

That drive to have an impact can be seen in her future goals. Makendra said it is important to her that she can help those who might not have much experience with technology — particularly small business owners and underserved communities. While her professional focus is on cybersecurity, she knows the experience she has gained through the School of Data Science will only help her in supporting others.

“I wanted my background to be a little bit of everything,” Makendra said. “With all my technical skills plus my volunteering, I feel like I will be able to connect with people more, especially those without technical experience.”

It is the same approach she built at the University, and one she plans to carry forward — using her skills to open doors, strengthen communities and make technology more accessible to the people who need it most.

[Hiring] Biology/Computational Biology - Summer Research Program @Novavax | Jobflarely

Jobflarely.← All Jobs

# [Hiring] Biology/Computational Biology - Summer Research Program @Novavax

Posted May 25, 2026

Role Description

We are seeking multiple highly motivated temporary employees to support cutting-edge research at the intersection of computational biology, artificial intelligence, and vaccine discovery. These positions offer a unique opportunity to be mentored by Novavax scientists while contributing to the computational design and experimental assessment of novel protein antigens by leveraging the latest advances in large language models (LLMs), GPT-based systems, and AI-driven protein engineering.

The contractors will work closely with a multidisciplinary team of computational scientists and experimental biologists to accelerate next-generation vaccine antigen discovery.

- Key Responsibilities
- Assist in the computational design of novel protein antigens for vaccine research.
- Apply and evaluate emerging AI/ML approaches, including large language models (LLMs) and GPT-based tools, for protein sequence and structure design.
- Support experimental assessment and analysis of designed protein antigens in collaboration with laboratory teams.
- Analyze and interpret computational and experimental data; document results and insights.
- Contribute to internal reports, presentations, and project discussions.
- Stay informed on recent advances in AI-driven protein design and computational biology.

- Qualifications
- Currently enrolled as a full-time undergraduate (Bachelors, B.S.) or Master’s student at a U.S. university during Summer 2026.
- Major field of study should be in Computer Science, Biology, Computational Biology, Bioinformatics, or a closely related field.
- Completion of at least 25% of required academic credits toward the degree program.
- Strong interest in vaccine research, protein design, or AI applications in biology.
- Basic proficiency in at least one programming language (e.g., Python, R, or similar).
- Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a research-focused environment.

- Requirements
- Coursework or experience in machine learning, deep learning, or AI.
- Familiarity with protein biology, structural biology, or bioinformatics tools.
- Experience with data analysis, scientific computing, or cloud-based workflows.
- Prior exposure to LLMs, GPTs, or generative models applied to scientific problems.

- Benefits
- Annual base salary (or hourly wage) falls within the range of $20.90 - $26.15.
- Base salary, annual bonus, equity grants, and professional career development/growth opportunities.
- Comprehensive benefits package including medical, dental, vision, Rx, STD, LTD, Life, Optional Life, 401(k) plan.

highlights:
Senior AI Developer | Jobflarely
[...]
# Senior AI Developer
[...]
Join a forward-thinking university team shaping the future of research with cutting-edge AI. As a Senior AI Developer, you’ll design and deploy secure, custom solutions powered by Large Language Models and RAG pipelines—helping researchers work smarter, faster, and more efficiently. From building high-performance vector databases to advancing ethical, institutionally grounded AI, your work will directly support innovation at scale. If you’re a skilled Python developer with hands-on LLM experience and a passion for turning complex technology into real-world impact, this is your opportunity to lead meaningful change in a collaborative academic environment.
[...]
- 
- The Senior AI Developer will be a member of the Office of the Vice President for Research & Innovation (OVPRI) IT team and is responsible for the design, development, and maintenance of customized artificial intelligence solutions that enhance the research mission of the University. The Developer will leverage Large Language Models (LLMs) and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to create secure tools that will accelerate and enhance the research administration process. The incumbent must balance high-level machine learning engineering with a deep commitment to digital ethics and institutional data sovereignty.
[...]
### LLM Development and Managmeent-
[...]
- 
- 

- Custom LLM Implementation: Design and maintain LLM models that allows the research community to access models via a secure, authenticated internal interface.

- RAG Pipeline Construction: Build and optimize Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipelines that allow AI agents to "read" university-specific research administration and regulatory affairs data (grant proposals, contracts, agreements, protocols, etc) without leaking that data into public training sets.

- Vector Database Management: Manage and scale vector databases to ensure high-speed semantic search across research administration and regulatory affairs documents.

- Document APIs to allow applications to utilize AI technologies developed

### Research AI Technologies -

-

- Minimum of 2 years of experience in software development with at least 1 year of hands-on experience developing applications using LLM APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic, or open-source models); or an equivalent combination of education and professional experience.

Generative AI - Senior Associate
[...]
Generative artificial intelligence is transforming how we build products, serve clients, and operate at scale. In the Chief Data and Analytics Office, you will help turn advanced models into dependable, secure, and high-performing production services. You will work with partners across machine learning, cloud engineering, and site reliability engineering to deliver solutions with clear return on investment. If you enjoy hands-on engineering, real-world constraints, and high-impact delivery, this role is for you. As a Senior Associate, Generative AI Engineer in the Chief Data and Analytics Office, you will help design, build, and support production generative artificial intelligence products and reusable backend application programming interfaces used across the firm. You will combine large enterprise datasets with large language and multimodal models to deliver scalable, measurable solutions. You will collaborate closely with machine learning, cloud engineering, and site reliability engineering partners to ensure reliability, performance, and strong operational controls.
